doc.title: Event - Web service Documentation
doc.div: The fdsnws/event web service return event (earthquake) information from our catalogs. The results are returned as XML in QuakeML format ( schema ), but may also be requested in text format.
To retrieve data from the event service, submit a request using the GET HTTP method.
PUT and DELETE methods are INGV extension to the standard, they need authorization and are not accessible to the public.
Dedicated clients that use the fdsnws-event service can be found on the IRIS DMC Client list page.
This service is an implementation of the FDSN event web service specification version 1.2
Below is a full list of service parameters and their usage.

method.name: GET
method.doc: Returns a list of available catalogs from which events can be selected.

method.name: GET
method.doc: Returns a list of contributors (agencies, etc.) across all catalogs from which events can be selected.

method.name: GET
method.doc: Get service description as application.wadl file.

method.name: GET
method.doc:
Get event in QuakeML format. Provide a series of parameter-value pairs in the URL. Each parameter-value pair must be separated from the others by an & and no space. A GET request may take one of the following general forms:
/query? [geographic-constraints] [depth-constraints] [temporal-constraints] [magnitude-constraints] [includeallmagnitudes] [includeallorigins] [includearrivals] [format-option] [nodata=404]
/query? (id-options) [includeallmagnitudes] [includeallorigins] [includearrivals] [format-option] [nodata=404]

param.name: starttime
param.doc: Limit to events on or after the specified start time.

param.name: endtime
param.doc: Limit to events on or before the specified end time.

param.name: updatedafter
param.doc: Limit to metadata updated after specified time; updates are data center specific.

param.name: minlatitude
param.doc: Specify southern boundary for search.

param.name: maxlatitude
param.doc: Specify northern boundary for search.

param.name: minlongitude
param.doc: Specify western boundary for search.

param.name: maxlongitude
param.doc: Specify eastern boundary for search.

param.name: latitude
param.doc: Specify the central latitude point for a radial search.

param.name: longitude
param.doc: Specify the central longitude point for a radial search.

param.name: minradius
param.doc: Specify minimum distance from the geographic point defined by latitude and longitude. Degrees.

param.name: maxradius
param.doc: Specify maximum distance from the geographic point defined by latitude and longitude. Degrees.

param.name: mindepth
param.doc: Limit to events with depth more than the specified minimum.

param.name: maxdepth
param.doc: Limit to events with depth less than the specified maximum.

param.name: minmagnitude
param.doc: Limit to events with magnitude more than the specified minimum.

param.name: maxmagnitude
param.doc: Limit to events with magnitude less than the specified maximum.

param.name: magnitudetype
param.doc: Specify a magnitude type to use for testing the minimum and maximum limits.

param.name: eventtype
param.doc: Limit to events with a specified eventType. The parameter value can be a single item, a comma-separated list of items. Allowed values are from QuakeML or unknown if eventType is not given.

param.name: includeallmagnitudes
param.doc: Specify if all magnitudes for the event should be included, default includes the preferred magnitude only.

param.name: includeallorigins
param.doc: Specify if all origins for the event should be included, default is data center dependent but is suggested to be the preferred origin only.

param.name: includearrivals
param.doc: Specify if phase arrivals should be included.

param.name: eventid
param.doc: Select a specific event by ID; event identifiers are data center specific.

param.name: limit
param.doc: Limit the results to the specified number of events.

param.name: orderby
param.doc: Order the result by time or magnitude, descending or ascending.

param.name: offset
param.doc: Return results starting at the event count specified, starting at 1.

param.name: minradiuskm
param.doc: Specify minimum distance from the geographic point defined by latitude and longitude. Kilometers. This is an x-fdsn-event extension to the FDSN specification.

param.name: maxradiuskm
param.doc: Specify maximum distance from the geographic point defined by latitude and longitude. Kilometers. This is an x-fdsn-event extension to the FDSN specification.

param.name: asofdate
param.doc: Limit to metadata current in database at date asofdate. This is an x-fdsn-event to the FDSN specification, optionally activated by the datacenter.

response.doc: Bad request due to improper specification, unrecognized parameter, duplicate identifier, etc..
Alternate to 204 (set via the 'nodata' parameter), normally used for results returned to a web browser.
Request would result in too much data being returned or the request itself is too large returned error message should include the service limitations in the detailed description.
Service temporarily unavailable, used in maintenance and error conditions

method.name: PUT
method.doc: Put event Quakeml file into database. Extension to fdsnws/event standard

param.name: filename
param.doc: Filename in format PROVIDER_CODE.xml.

param.name: dbname
param.doc: Destination database name.

param.name: upindex
param.doc: Optionally updates indexes.

response.doc: Bad request due to improper specification, unrecognized parameter, duplicate identifier, etc..
Authentication failed or access blocked to restricted data.
Service temporarily unavailable, used in maintenance and error conditions
